tests/fixtures/material_pgp_fixture.pgp — reproduction note
===========================================================

This file is a hand-crafted Ed25519-primary OpenPGP public key, generated once,
bytes committed. We do NOT regenerate at CI time because Ed25519 key generation
uses OS randomness — same recipe produces a different fixture each run (Name/
Email deterministic; key bytes + signature bytes random).

Deterministic content:
  Primary algorithm:  Ed25519 (RFC 4880 §13.2 / RFC 9580 §5.5.5.5; algo ID 22 EDDSA, KeyType::Ed25519Legacy in rpgp 0.19.0)
  Primary UID:        cp <f@cp.t>             (10 chars — under D-P7-03 + GAP 5 ≤20-char budget)
  Subkeys:            none
  Expiration:         none (no expiration subpacket)
  Packet version:     v4 (40-hex SHA-1 fingerprint; RFC 4880)
  Self-cert subpkts:  rpgp 0.19.0 minimal defaults — issuer fingerprint, sig-creation, key-flags 0x01,
                      features 0x01, primary-user-id flag, plus EMPTY pref-sym/hash/zip/aead arrays.

Generation recipe (Plan 04 path 2 — pure rpgp; Path 1 gpg fallback documented below):

  cargo run --example generate_pgp_fixture
  # Writes:
  #   tests/fixtures/material_pgp_fixture.pgp           (~202 B, public TPK)
  #   tests/fixtures/material_pgp_secret_fixture.pgp    (~239 B, secret TSK)

Examples source: examples/generate_pgp_fixture.rs (committed; uses
SecretKeyParamsBuilder + KeyType::Ed25519Legacy + can_certify(true) +
EncryptionCaps::None + zero subkeys + UID "cp <f@cp.t>").

Why rpgp instead of gpg (Plan 04 deviation):
  gpg --batch --generate-key with the equivalent EDDSA recipe produces a 215 B
  stream because gpg includes preference subpackets (pref-sym 9 8 7 2,
  pref-hash 10 9 8 11 2, pref-zip 2 3 1, pref-aead 2) that bloat the self-cert.
  rpgp's defaults emit empty preference arrays — saving 13 B. Both fixtures
  exceed the encoded 1000 B PKARR BEP44 budget regardless (see "Wire-budget"
  note below); choosing rpgp is the smaller-of-two evils for tightness.

Verification (deterministic on parser state, not signature bytes):

  gpg --list-packets < tests/fixtures/material_pgp_fixture.pgp
  # ->  :public key packet: algo 22 (EDDSA), version 4
  #     :user ID packet: "cp <f@cp.t>"
  #     :signature packet: algo 22 (EDDSA), digest algo 8 (SHA-256), sigclass 0x13

Fixture sizes (Phase 7 research GAP 5 + D-P7-03):
  Public fixture (committed):    202 bytes
  Secret fixture (committed):    239 bytes
  Realistic fixture (committed): 935 bytes  — used by WireBudgetExceeded test
  JCS envelope fixture:          376 bytes  — material_pgp_signable.bin

Wire-budget reality (Plan 04 measurement, supersedes research GAP-5 prediction):
  Research predicted: raw × 4.16 ≈ encoded → 202 × 4.16 ≈ 840 B (fits under 1000)
  Actual measured:    encoded = 1236 B (encoded/raw ≈ 6.1×)
  Plaintext after JCS envelope wrapping: 383 B (envelope adds ~180 B framing for
  the {created_at, material:{type:pgp_key,bytes:b64}, protocol_version, purpose}
  structure plus the base64 expansion of the bytes field).
  CONSEQUENCE: pgp_self_round_trip_recovers_packet_stream is `#[ignore]`'d per
  the same SSH-style fallback that D-P7-03 carved for OpenSSH. The positive
  WireBudgetExceeded test still ships and proves the error surface is clean.
  Re-enable when v1.2 two-tier storage lands.

SHA-256 of the committed files (recorded at generation time, for tamper
detection during reviews):
  public:    031d7e29dcdbba3682dec896321b7f71a5e24411f6e8cb75a3016df024c422ff
  secret:    95294cd6c46a94208e81554d727b61e126bd0199867214d6b6b7281efe361d4a
  realistic: cfa41efbebac4e53a6417ea842860d132a524c39ca74e19f9373c7488601e81e

(The SHA-256 above will MISMATCH on regeneration because Ed25519 / RSA key bytes
are random — the values are recorded for forensic tamper detection across
reviewers working with the SAME committed bytes, not as a regeneration target.)

Realistic-overflow fixture: tests/fixtures/material_pgp_fixture_realistic.pgp
  Recipe (Path 1 gpg — used because rpgp's RSA keygen is slower):

    export GNUPGHOME=$(mktemp -d)
    cat > /tmp/batch <<EOF
    %no-protection
    %no-ask-passphrase
    Key-Type: RSA
    Key-Length: 3072
    Name-Real: cipherpost-fixture-realistic
    Name-Email: realistic-overflow@cipherpost.test
    Expire-Date: 0
    %commit
    EOF
    gpg --batch --generate-key /tmp/batch
    KEYID=$(gpg --list-keys --with-colons | awk -F: '/^pub/ {print $5; exit}')
    gpg --export "$KEYID" > tests/fixtures/material_pgp_fixture_realistic.pgp

  Purpose: drives Error::WireBudgetExceeded positive test (D-P7-02). RSA-3072
           with a long UID guarantees raw size > 245 B → encoded > 1020 B.

Secret-key fixture: tests/fixtures/material_pgp_secret_fixture.pgp
  Generated by the same `cargo run --example generate_pgp_fixture` invocation
  (the example writes both TPK + TSK from the single SignedSecretKey it builds).
  Purpose: golden-string test for [WARNING: SECRET key] line placement
           (preview::render_pgp_preview tag-5 path; D-P7-07).

JCS envelope fixture: tests/fixtures/material_pgp_signable.bin
  Regenerate via:
    cargo test --test material_pgp_envelope_round_trip -- --ignored regenerate_material_pgp_envelope_fixture
  Then commit the new bytes; the byte-identity assertion will lock the new shape.

Path 1 (gpg, deprecated for the public/secret fixtures — kept for the realistic
overflow fixture only):
  This produces a 215 B stream (vs rpgp's 202) due to gpg's default preference
  subpackets. Recipe:

    export GNUPGHOME=$(mktemp -d)
    cat > /tmp/batch <<EOF
    %no-protection
    %no-ask-passphrase
    Key-Type: EDDSA
    Key-Curve: ed25519
    Name-Real: cp
    Name-Email: f@cp.t
    Expire-Date: 0
    %commit
    EOF
    gpg --batch --generate-key /tmp/batch
    KEYID=$(gpg --list-keys --with-colons | awk -F: '/^pub/ {print $5; exit}')
    gpg --export "$KEYID" > tests/fixtures/material_pgp_fixture.pgp
    gpg --export-secret-keys "$KEYID" > tests/fixtures/material_pgp_secret_fixture.pgp
